title: ARK: Survival — getting started
body:
Your ReFx Hosting ARK server installs automatically, so this guide covers connecting, naming your session, becoming admin, tuning common settings, and adding mods.

## First boot

ARK is a Steam-based game, so it installs itself the moment your server provisions. The first start can take a little while as it downloads game files, so give it a few minutes.

1. Open **My Servers** and click your ARK server.
2. Go to the **Console** tab and press **Start**.
3. Watch the live output until it reports the server is ready.

> Tip: If the server seems stuck on its very first start, that's usually the initial download finishing. Let it run before troubleshooting.

## Connect to your server

Find your server address and port in the panel — see [Finding and connecting to your server](/knowledge-base/finding-and-connecting-to-your-server). In ARK, the easiest way to join is the in-game server browser using the **Unofficial** or **Favorites** filter, or by adding your address directly to favorites in Steam.

## Name your session and set passwords

Your session name is what players see in the browser. You can set the session name, a join password, and a separate admin password through the panel's configuration fields, the **Files** tab, or in-game/console commands.

- **Session name** — the public name of your server.
- **Server password** — optional; require it to keep the server private.
- **Admin password** — used to grant yourself admin powers in-game.

After changing any of these, restart the server from the **Console** tab so the new values take effect.

## Become admin

Once an admin password is set, you can elevate yourself in-game:

1. Join your server.
2. Open the console (typically the **Tab** key on PC).
3. Type `enablecheats YourAdminPassword`.

You now have admin access for that session. Use ARK's admin commands to spawn items, manage players, and adjust the world.

## Adjust common settings

Most gameplay options — max players/slots, difficulty, XP and harvest rates, day/night cycle, PvP/PvE, and similar — are set through panel configuration fields, the **Files** tab, or console commands. Make your changes, then restart the server to apply them.

> Note: Bigger rate and slot changes can affect performance. If things feel sluggish, see [Reducing lag and performance](/knowledge-base/reducing-lag-and-performance).

## Add mods

ARK supports Steam Workshop content. Add mods by their **mod IDs** (or via a Workshop tab where available), then restart so the server downloads and loads them.

1. Find the mod IDs for the content you want.
2. Add them in the panel as described in [Installing mods and Workshop content](/knowledge-base/installing-mods-and-workshop).
3. Restart the server and confirm the mods load in the console output.

Players generally need the same mods enabled to join a modded server.

---

Want to back up your world before experimenting? See [Creating and restoring backups](/knowledge-base/creating-and-restoring-backups). You can also schedule timed restarts under [Scheduling tasks](/knowledge-base/scheduling-tasks).

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Canceling a server & refunds
body:
When you no longer need a server, you can stop its automatic renewals and let it lapse, and this article explains how, what happens to your data, and where to find our refund policy.

## How billing works first

Every server at ReFx Hosting renews automatically on the billing interval you chose at checkout (Weekly, Biweekly, Monthly, Quarterly, 6 months, or Yearly). On each renewal date, an invoice is raised and paid with your saved payment method or account credit. You can review all of this under **Billing** in the left nav.

To stop paying for a server, you stop those renewals from continuing.

## Letting a server lapse

The simplest way to part with a server is to let its current term run out without renewing:

1. Make sure you have everything you want to keep (see the data note below).
2. Remove or replace the saved payment method you don't want charged, or plan to leave the renewal invoice unpaid.
3. When the next invoice goes unpaid, the server is **suspended** until it is paid.
4. While it stays unpaid, the server is no longer active and you stop being billed for it.

> Note: A suspended server is paused, not deleted right away. If you change your mind, pay the outstanding invoice under **Billing** to bring it back online.

## What happens to your data

Once a server is suspended, you lose access to its console, files, and panel tools, and anything stored only on that server can become unavailable. Back it up first.

Before you cancel:

1. Open the server, go to the **Backups** tab to create a fresh backup, then download it. See [Creating and restoring backups](/knowledge-base/creating-and-restoring-backups).
2. Or pull your files off via the **Files** tab or SFTP. See [Managing files and SFTP](/knowledge-base/managing-files-and-sftp).

> Tip: If you only want a different game, you may not need to cancel at all. With game switching, a server keeps its address, SFTP, backups, and subscription while you swap the game. See [Switching games on your server](/knowledge-base/switching-games-on-your-server).

## Downsizing instead of canceling

If the server is fine but the plan is too big, downgrade rather than cancel. Use the **Upgrade** tab to move to a smaller plan, tier, or slot count. Downgrades take effect at your next renewal, lowering what you pay going forward. See [Upgrading or downgrading your plan](/knowledge-base/upgrading-or-downgrading-your-plan).

## Refunds

Whether a payment is eligible for a refund depends on our refund policy and the time since purchase. Please read the full terms before requesting one:

- Refund policy: https://refx.gg/refunds

To request a refund or confirm whether yours qualifies, open a ticket from **Support** with the relevant invoice details so our team can review it.

---

Related help: [Invoices, renewals & payment](/knowledge-base/invoices-renewals-and-payment) and [Coupons, gift cards & credit](/knowledge-base/coupons-gift-cards-and-credit).

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: I can’t connect to my server
body:
If you can't reach your server, the fix is almost always one of a few quick checks — let's walk through them in order.

## 1. Make sure the server is actually running

Open your server in the panel and check the **Console** tab.

1. Look at whether the status shows the server as running or stopped.
2. If it's stopped, press **Start** and watch the live console output.
3. Give it a moment — some games take a while to fully load before they accept connections.

If the server won't start or stops on its own, that's a different problem. See [My server won't start](/knowledge-base/server-wont-start).

## 2. Use the exact address and port from the panel

This is the most common cause. Always copy the connection details straight from the panel rather than typing them from memory.

- Use the full address and port shown on your server's page (for voice servers, the **Voice** tab; for web hosting, the address on your site's page).
- Don't drop or change the port number — many games and TeamSpeak need it.
- Paste it exactly into your game client, TeamSpeak, or browser.

> Tip: The address can change if you switch nodes or regions, so re-check the panel rather than reusing an old saved entry.

## 3. Check whitelist, password, and access settings

If the server is up but rejecting you specifically:

- **Whitelist:** if you've enabled a whitelist, make sure your account or name is on it. For Minecraft, manage this via the console or files — see [Minecraft: getting started](/knowledge-base/minecraft-getting-started).
- **Password:** confirm the server password (and admin password, if any) matches what's set in your panel configuration or files.
- **Slots full:** if every slot is taken, you'll be turned away until someone leaves.

## 4. Check your own side

Sometimes the issue isn't the server at all:

- **Game or client version:** your client must match the server's version. Update your game, or set the server to the version you're running.
- **Your firewall or network:** a local firewall, antivirus, school or work network, or VPN can block game and voice ports. Try a different network to rule this out.
- **Restart your client:** close and reopen the game, TeamSpeak, or browser, then reconnect.
- **TeamSpeak license:** a voice server won't run until you accept the TeamSpeak license once on the **Voice** tab — see [TeamSpeak: getting started](/knowledge-base/teamspeak-getting-started).

## 5. Check billing

A suspended server won't accept connections. Check **Billing** for any unpaid invoice and pay it to restore service — see [Invoices, renewals, and payment](/knowledge-base/invoices-renewals-and-payment).

---

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Changing your password & email
body:
Keeping your password and email up to date is one of the simplest ways to keep your ReFx Hosting account secure and reachable.

You manage both from the **Account** page in the left navigation of your panel.

## Changing your password

1. Open **Account** from the left navigation.
2. Find the password section and enter your current password.
3. Enter your new password, then confirm it.
4. Save your changes.

We recommend a **strong, unique password** that you do not reuse on any other website. A longer passphrase made of several random words is easy to remember and hard to guess.

> Tip: A password manager can generate and store a long random password for you, so you never have to type or memorize it.

## Changing your email address

Your email is where invoices, renewal reminders, and support replies are sent, so keep it current.

1. Open **Account** from the left navigation.
2. Enter your new email address.
3. Save your changes.

After changing your email, you will need to **re-verify** it. We send a verification message to the new address, so open it and follow the link to confirm.

> Note: Until the new email is verified, some actions like placing a new order may be unavailable. See [Verifying your email](/knowledge-base/verifying-your-email) if the message does not arrive or the link has expired.

## Add a second layer with 2FA

A strong password is a great start, but two-factor authentication (2FA) protects your account even if your password is ever exposed. ReFx Hosting supports:

- An **authenticator app** (TOTP) that generates a rotating code.
- A **passkey** (WebAuthn) using your device biometrics or a security key.

Turning on 2FA takes a couple of minutes and is the single best thing you can do to keep your servers safe. Walk through setup in [Securing your account with 2FA](/knowledge-base/securing-your-account-2fa).

---

## Good habits

- Change your password right away if you suspect anyone else has seen it.
- Never share your main login. To let someone help with a single server, invite them as a [sub-user](/knowledge-base/sub-users-and-permissions) with limited permissions instead.
- For automation and scripts, create a scoped [API key](/knowledge-base/using-api-keys) rather than handing out your password.

If you have lost access to your email or 2FA, see [Recovering your account](/knowledge-base/recovering-your-account).

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Choosing a web hosting plan
body:
ReFx Hosting web plans range from Starter up to Pro, and the right fit comes down to how much traffic, storage, and power your site needs — and you can always upgrade later.

## How the plans compare

Every web hosting plan gives you the same core experience: a file manager and SFTP for uploading your site, the option to add a custom domain with automatic SSL, fast NVMe storage, DDoS protection, and one unified panel. What changes as you move up is the amount of **CPU, RAM, and disk** you get.

- **Starter** — The entry tier, ideal for small personal sites, portfolios, and blogs with light traffic. Plenty for a simple HTML/CSS/JS site you want online quickly.
- **Pro** — The top tier, with the most CPU, RAM, and disk, for higher-traffic sites and projects that demand the most performance.

Plans in between step up the resources gradually, so you can pick the size that matches where your site is today.

## Which one fits me?

1. **A blog or personal page?** Start with **Starter** — it covers the essentials at the lowest cost.
2. **A growing site getting more visitors?** Move up a tier for extra headroom.
3. **A busier project or business site?** Choose a mid or higher plan for stronger, steadier performance.
4. **A high-traffic site?** Pick **Pro** for the most CPU, RAM, and disk.

> Tip: Not sure how much you need? Start smaller. You're never locked in — moving up takes just a few clicks.

## Upgrade anytime

You don't have to predict your growth perfectly. From your server's **Upgrade** tab you can switch to a higher (or lower) plan whenever you like.

When you upgrade mid-cycle, you'll see a small **prorated invoice** to apply the change immediately. Downgrades take effect at your next renewal. Your site, files, and custom domain stay exactly where they are — only the resources change. See [Upgrading or downgrading your plan](/knowledge-base/upgrading-or-downgrading-your-plan) for the full walkthrough.

---

## Related articles

- [Web hosting: getting started](/knowledge-base/web-hosting-getting-started)
- [Connecting a custom domain](/knowledge-base/connecting-a-custom-domain)
- [Understanding server resources](/knowledge-base/understanding-server-resources)

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Connecting a custom domain (with free SSL)
body:
Point your own domain at your ReFx Hosting web hosting and get a free SSL certificate issued automatically.

By default your site is reachable on the address shown in the panel, but you can connect a domain you own (like `yourname.com`) so visitors see your brand instead. Here's how.

## Before you start

You'll need:

- An active **Web Hosting** plan with ReFx Hosting (see [Web Hosting: getting started](/knowledge-base/web-hosting-getting-started)).
- A domain you've already registered with a domain registrar.
- Access to that domain's DNS settings at your registrar.

## Step 1: Add your domain in the panel

1. Open **My Servers** and select your web hosting server.
2. Add a custom domain in the panel.
3. Type your domain exactly (for example `yourname.com`) and save.

The panel then shows you the **IP address** your domain needs to point at. Keep it open — you'll need it next.

## Step 2: Create the DNS record

Log in at your domain registrar and open its DNS management page. Create a record that points your domain at the IP shown in the panel:

- Use an **A record** for an IPv4 address (the most common case).
- Use an **AAAA record** if the panel shows an IPv6 address.
- Set the record's **value** (sometimes called "points to" or "target") to the exact IP from the panel.
- For the root domain, the host/name field is usually `@`. For a subdomain like `www`, enter `www`.

Save the record at your registrar.

> Note: Each registrar names things slightly differently, but the goal is always the same — an A (or AAAA) record whose value is the IP shown in your panel.

## Step 3: Wait for DNS to propagate

DNS changes don't take effect instantly. Your new record needs time to spread across the internet — this is called **propagation**. It often completes within minutes but can take up to 24-48 hours.

During this time the panel checks whether your domain is pointing correctly. Once it verifies, your domain is connected.

## Step 4: SSL is issued automatically

Once your domain verifies, ReFx Hosting issues a free **SSL certificate** for it automatically — no extra steps, no cost. That's what puts the padlock in the browser and serves your site over `https://`. The certificate is also **renewed automatically**, so you never have to think about it again.

---

## Troubleshooting

- **Still showing "pending" or not verifying?** Double-check the IP in the DNS record matches the panel exactly, and give propagation more time.
- **Typo in the record?** Fix it at your registrar — only one A/AAAA record should point the domain at your hosting IP.
- **Site loads but no padlock yet?** SSL is issued after the domain verifies, so wait a little longer once propagation finishes.

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Coupons, gift cards & account credit
body:
Coupons, gift cards, and account credit all save you money at checkout — here's how each one works and where to find your balances.

## The three ways to save

ReFx Hosting gives you three things you can apply when you order or renew:

- **Coupon code** — a promotional code that takes a discount off your order.
- **Gift card** — a prepaid code you redeem to top up your account credit.
- **Account credit** — a balance on your account that's available to spend at checkout.

## Applying a coupon code

You apply a coupon during checkout, after you've chosen your game or plan, hardware tier or slots, billing interval, and location.

1. Go to **Order**, build your server, and continue to the payment step.
2. Find the coupon / promo code field.
3. Enter your code and apply it.
4. The order total updates to reflect the discount.
5. Finish paying by card or PayPal as usual.

> Tip: Enter the coupon before you pay so the discount is reflected in the amount charged.

## Redeeming a gift card

Redeeming a gift card adds its value to your **account credit**, which you can then spend on any order or renewal.

1. Go to **Billing**.
2. Find the gift card / redeem option.
3. Enter your gift card code and redeem it.
4. The value is added to your account credit balance.

Once redeemed, you don't need the code again — the value lives in your credit balance.

## Using account credit at checkout

Account credit is the simplest to use because it's already on your account.

1. Build your order under **Order**, or open an invoice under **Billing**.
2. At the payment step, choose to apply account credit.
3. Credit is put toward the total; pay any remaining balance by card or PayPal.

If your credit covers the whole amount, there may be nothing left to pay.

## Where to see your balances

Everything lives under **Billing**:

- Your current **account credit** balance is shown there.
- **Invoices** show what you were charged and any discounts or credit applied.
- You can also manage payment methods and pay outstanding invoices from the same place.

For a full walkthrough of invoices and automatic renewals, see [Invoices, renewals & payment](/knowledge-base/invoices-renewals-and-payment).

---

## Good to know

- Account credit also applies to **automatic renewals**, helping cover the next invoice on your server's billing interval.
- Choosing a longer billing term (Monthly, Quarterly, 6 months, or Yearly) lowers your per-month cost — a separate way to save alongside coupons and credit. See [Upgrading or downgrading your plan](/knowledge-base/upgrading-or-downgrading-your-plan).

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Creating & restoring backups
body:
Backups are your safety net — they save a snapshot of your server so you can roll back if something goes wrong.

You'll find everything in the **Backups** tab of your server's control panel. From there you can create a backup on demand, schedule automatic backups, restore a snapshot, and download a copy to keep on your own computer.

> Tip: Always take a backup **before** big changes — installing mods, updating versions, editing config files, or [switching the game your server runs](/knowledge-base/switching-games-on-your-server). A two-minute backup can save you hours.

## Create a backup on demand

1. Open your server and go to the **Backups** tab.
2. Click **Create backup**.
3. Give it a clear name (for example, `before-modpack-update`) so you can find it later.
4. Wait for it to finish — larger servers take a little longer.

When it's done, the backup appears in your list with its name and date.

## Schedule automatic backups

Regular, hands-off backups mean you're always covered without having to remember.

1. In the **Backups** tab, set up an automatic backup schedule.
2. Choose how often it should run.
3. Save it — ReFx Hosting then creates backups for you automatically.

You can also automate backups from the **Schedules** tab as part of a wider routine. See [Scheduling tasks](/knowledge-base/scheduling-tasks) for more.

## Restore a backup

Restoring rolls your server back to exactly how it was when that snapshot was taken.

1. Go to the **Backups** tab and find the backup you want.
2. Click **Restore**.
3. Confirm — this replaces your current server files with the backed-up version.

> Note: It's safest to **stop your server** before restoring so files aren't changing at the same time. Restoring overwrites current data, so take a fresh backup first if there's anything recent you want to keep.

## Download a backup

Want an off-panel copy? You can download any backup to your own computer for safekeeping.

1. Open the **Backups** tab.
2. Find the backup you want.
3. Click **Download** and save the file locally.

A downloaded copy is great extra protection for worlds, configs, or anything you can't easily recreate.

## A few good habits

- Back up **before** updates, mod changes, version changes, or game switches.
- Use **clear names** so you know what each snapshot is.
- Keep a **scheduled** backup running for everyday peace of mind.
- **Download** important snapshots so you have a copy outside the panel.

---

Backups travel with your server's identity, so even if you swap the game it runs, your saved snapshots stay with it. Learn more in [Switching games on your server](/knowledge-base/switching-games-on-your-server).

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Editing startup parameters & variables
body:
The Startup tab lets you fine-tune how your server launches by editing its startup command and a set of server variables, right from the panel.

## What the Startup tab does

Every server starts with a **startup command** and a list of **variables** that control how it runs. The Startup tab in your server's control panel shows both in one place, so you can adjust how the server boots without editing files by hand.

- The **startup command** is the instruction that launches your game or app. Most of the time you can leave it alone.
- **Variables** are the editable values plugged into that command — things like which version to run, how much memory to use, or a specific build or branch.

What you see here depends on the server type and the game you're running, so the exact fields vary from product to product.

## Common variables you might change

You won't need all of these, but these are the kinds of values that commonly appear:

- **Game version or build** — pick which version your server runs (for example, a specific Minecraft version).
- **Loader or distribution** — for Minecraft, the loader (Vanilla, Paper, Fabric, Forge, NeoForge) may be selected here. See [Minecraft: getting started](/knowledge-base/minecraft-getting-started).
- **Memory or allocation** — how much of your plan's resources the server uses on launch.
- **Branch or beta flags** — some Steam-based games let you opt into a beta or a specific branch.
- **World, map, or modpack** — point the server at a particular world, map, or modpack.

> Tip: Many in-game settings (server name, password, max players, RCON) are **not** on the Startup tab. They live in your game's configuration fields or the [Files](/knowledge-base/managing-files-and-sftp) tab instead.

## Saving changes safely

Startup changes don't always take effect instantly, so plan a quick interruption:

1. Stop the server from the [Console](/knowledge-base/using-the-live-console) tab.
2. Open the **Startup** tab and edit the command or variables you need.
3. Save your changes.
4. Start the server again and watch the console to confirm it boots cleanly.

> Note: Some changes, such as switching version or loader, may need a reinstall to fully apply. If your server won't boot after an edit, see [Reinstalling your server](/knowledge-base/reinstalling-your-server). A reinstall can affect server files, so take a [backup](/knowledge-base/creating-and-restoring-backups) first.

## If something goes wrong

If you save a value and the server refuses to start, the safest fix is to undo your last change and restart. A bad version, an invalid branch name, or too much memory for your plan are common culprits.

- Check the console output for the error message — it usually names the problem.
- Revert the variable you just changed, then start again.
- Still stuck? See [Server won't start](/knowledge-base/server-wont-start).

---

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Finding & connecting to your server
body:
Once your server is set up, you can find it under My Servers, copy its address, and connect from your game client in just a few clicks.

## Find your server

Every server you rent lives in one place in your ReFx Hosting panel.

1. Log in to your account.
2. In the left navigation, click **My Servers**.
3. Click the server you want to manage to open its control panel.

Setup is instant, so a new server should appear here within moments of your payment clearing. Don't see it? It may still be finishing up, or your order may need a verified email first — see [Verifying your email](/knowledge-base/verifying-your-email).

## Copy your server address

Your server's address and port are shown in the panel, usually on the **Console** tab or near the top of the server screen.

- Copy the full address, including the port if one is shown.
- For most games, paste this into your game client's "Add Server" or "Direct Connect" field.
- For TeamSpeak, use the voice address and port shown on the **Voice** tab.
- For web hosting, your site goes live on the address shown in the panel.

> Tip: Keep the panel open in a browser tab while you connect, so the exact address and port are always one copy away.

## Connect from your game

Connecting works just like joining any other server:

1. Start the game and open its multiplayer or server browser screen.
2. Choose **Direct Connect** or **Add Server**.
3. Paste in the address (and port) from your panel.
4. Join the server.

Make sure your server is running first — open the **Console** tab and press **Start** if it's stopped. You can watch the live output there to confirm it has finished loading before you connect. For more, see [Using the live console](/knowledge-base/using-the-live-console).

Game-specific setup like passwords, slots, or admin access is covered in each game's getting-started article, such as [Minecraft](/knowledge-base/minecraft-getting-started), [Rust](/knowledge-base/rust-getting-started), [ARK](/knowledge-base/ark-getting-started), [Valheim](/knowledge-base/valheim-getting-started), and [Palworld](/knowledge-base/palworld-getting-started).

## Find your SFTP details

To upload or download files with a desktop client like FileZilla or WinSCP, you'll use SFTP. The details you need are shown right in the panel.

1. Open your server and go to the **Files** tab.
2. Find the SFTP section, which shows the **host**, **port**, **username**, and **password**.
3. Enter those into your SFTP client and connect.

You can also browse, edit, upload, and delete files directly in the in-browser file manager — no extra software needed. Full details are in [Managing files and SFTP](/knowledge-base/managing-files-and-sftp).

---

## Trouble connecting?

If you've pasted the right address but still can't get in, double-check that the server is running and that you've included the correct port. Our walkthrough covers the most common fixes: [Can't connect to your server](/knowledge-base/cant-connect-to-server).

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Frequently asked questions
body:
Quick answers to the questions we hear most often about ordering, running, and managing your ReFx Hosting servers.

## Getting started

### How fast is setup?

Setup is instant. Once your payment clears, your server provisions automatically and appears under **My Servers** within moments — no waiting on manual approval. See [How to order a server](/knowledge-base/how-to-order-a-server).

### Do I need to verify my email before ordering?

Yes. Email verification is required before you can place an order. If the link hasn't arrived, see [Verifying your email](/knowledge-base/verifying-your-email).

### Can I have more than one server?

Absolutely. Order as many servers as you like — game, voice, and web hosting — and manage them all from one account and one panel under **My Servers**.

---

## Servers and games

### Can I switch which game my server runs?

Yes — it's one of our favorite features. Your server keeps its identity (its address, SFTP, backups, and subscription) while you swap the game it runs. Stop the server, open the game switcher, pick a new game, and choose to keep or wipe existing data. Available games depend on your plan. Full guide: [Switching games on your server](/knowledge-base/switching-games-on-your-server).

### Can I run mods and plugins?

Yes. Minecraft supports loaders like Paper (plugins) and Fabric, Forge, and NeoForge (mods), plus one-click modpack installs — see [Minecraft mods, plugins, and modpacks](/knowledge-base/minecraft-mods-plugins-modpacks). Many Steam-based games such as Rust and ARK support Steam Workshop content or mod IDs — see [Installing mods and Workshop content](/knowledge-base/installing-mods-and-workshop).

### Where are your servers located?

We offer multiple locations. At checkout you choose a region (and optionally a specific node) so you can put your server close to your players for the lowest latency.

---

## Billing

### How does billing work?

Servers renew automatically on the interval you chose (weekly through yearly — longer terms cost less per month). Invoices appear under **Billing**, where you can pay them, manage payment methods, and view account credit. Unpaid invoices can lead to a server being suspended until paid. See [Invoices, renewals, and payment](/knowledge-base/invoices-renewals-and-payment).

### Can I upgrade or downgrade later?

Yes. Use the **Upgrade** tab to change plan, tier, or slot count. Upgrades raise a prorated invoice and apply immediately once paid; downgrades take effect at your next renewal. See [Upgrading or downgrading your plan](/knowledge-base/upgrading-or-downgrading-your-plan).

### Can I use a coupon, gift card, or credit?

Yes — apply a coupon code, redeem a gift card, or spend account credit at checkout. See [Coupons, gift cards, and credit](/knowledge-base/coupons-gift-cards-and-credit).

### Do you offer refunds?

For details on canceling and what may be refundable, read [Canceling a server and refunds](/knowledge-base/canceling-a-server-and-refunds).

---

## Security and support

### Is there DDoS protection?

Yes. Your servers run with DDoS protection, fast NVMe storage, and instant setup as standard.

### How do I keep my account secure?

Add two-factor authentication — an authenticator app (TOTP) and/or a passkey — under **Account**. See [Securing your account with 2FA](/knowledge-base/securing-your-account-2fa).

### How do I get support?

The Knowledge Base is the fastest place to look. If you're still stuck, open a support ticket from the **Support** tab and our team will help.

> **Tip:** Set a priority on your ticket so we can route urgent issues quickly. Inactive tickets close automatically after a while.

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Getting started with ReFx Hosting
body:
Welcome to ReFx Hosting — this guide takes you from creating your account to connecting to your very first server.

ReFx Hosting lets you rent and manage game servers, TeamSpeak voice servers, and web hosting from one account and one control panel. Setup is instant: once your payment clears, your server is provisioned automatically and ready to go.

## 1. Create and verify your account

Sign up with your email address and a password. Before you can place an order, you need to verify your email so we know it's really you.

1. Sign up on the ReFx Hosting site.
2. Check your inbox for the verification email and click the link inside.
3. Once verified, you're ready to order.

> Tip: Can't find the email? Check your spam folder. See [Verifying your email](/knowledge-base/verifying-your-email) if you're stuck.

## 2. Order your first server

Head to the **Order** tab in the left-hand navigation. The catalog has three tabs so you can find exactly what you want:

- **Game Servers** — Minecraft, Rust, ARK, Valheim, Palworld, and more.
- **Voice Servers** — TeamSpeak, priced by slot capacity.
- **Web Hosting** — host a website, from Starter to Pro.

When you pick a product, you'll choose a hardware tier (or slot count for some products), a billing interval, a server location, and a name. Longer billing terms cost less per month, so they're worth considering if you plan to stick around.

Pay by card (Stripe) or PayPal at checkout. You can also apply a coupon code, redeem a gift card, or use account credit.

For a full walkthrough, read [How to order a server](/knowledge-base/how-to-order-a-server).

## 3. Find your server in My Servers

After payment clears, your server sets up automatically and appears under **My Servers** in the left nav. Click it to open its control panel.

The panel is where you'll do everything — view the live console, manage files, create backups, and more. New to it? Take the [Panel tour](/knowledge-base/panel-tour) to get oriented.

## 4. Connect and play

Your server's address and port are shown in the panel. Use them to connect from your game, your TeamSpeak client, or a web browser.

See [Finding and connecting to your server](/knowledge-base/finding-and-connecting-to-your-server) for connection details by server type.

---

## What makes ReFx different

One standout feature is **game switching**: your server keeps its identity — its address, SFTP, backups, and subscription — while you swap which game it runs. Want to move from Minecraft to Rust without buying a second server? You can. Learn how in [Switching games on your server](/knowledge-base/switching-games-on-your-server).

You also get instant setup, DDoS protection, fast NVMe storage, multiple locations, and one unified panel and billing for everything you rent.

## Next steps

- Lock down your account with [two-factor authentication](/knowledge-base/securing-your-account-2fa).
- Browse the [Frequently asked questions](/knowledge-base/frequently-asked-questions).
- Read [Invoices, renewals, and payment](/knowledge-base/invoices-renewals-and-payment) to understand billing.

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Installing mods & Steam Workshop content
body:
Adding mods lets you customize gameplay, and on supported games ReFx Hosting makes it straightforward through the panel.

## Before you start: back up

Mods change how your server runs, and a bad combination can stop it from starting. Always take a fresh backup first so you can roll back if something goes wrong.

1. Open your server and go to the **Backups** tab.
2. Create an on-demand backup and wait for it to finish.
3. Now you're safe to add or change mods.

See [Creating and restoring backups](/knowledge-base/creating-and-restoring-backups) for the full walkthrough.

## How mods get added

The method depends on the game, but it's usually one of these:

- **A Workshop tab** — many Steam-based games (such as Rust and ARK) include a Workshop tab in the panel where you add Steam Workshop items so they install automatically.
- **Mod IDs** — some games take a list of Workshop or mod IDs in a panel configuration field. Paste the IDs, save, and restart.
- **Minecraft loaders** — Minecraft uses loaders instead of the Workshop. Paper supports plugins; Fabric, Forge, and NeoForge support mods, and there's one-click modpack install. See [Minecraft mods, plugins & modpacks](/knowledge-base/minecraft-mods-plugins-modpacks).

> Not every game supports mods. If yours does, you'll see a Workshop tab or a mods field in the panel.

## Step by step (Workshop / mod IDs)

1. **Stop the server** from the **Console** tab so changes apply cleanly.
2. Open the **Workshop** tab, or the relevant field on the **Startup** or configuration screen.
3. Add the Workshop items or paste the **mod IDs** you want.
4. **Save** your changes.
5. **Start the server** again — supported mods download and install automatically on boot. Larger mods can make the first start take a little longer.
6. Check the **Console** output to confirm everything loaded without errors.

## Match your versions

Most mod problems come down to mismatched versions. A few things to keep in mind:

- Use mods built for the **same game version** your server is running.
- When using a **modpack or a set of mods**, make sure they're meant to work together.
- Players usually need the **same mods installed locally** to join a modded server — share your mod list with them.

## Restart to apply

Adding or removing a mod only takes effect after a restart. Any time you change your mod list, save in the panel and then restart from the **Console** tab. If you remove mods, restart as well so the server loads a clean list.

---

## If the server won't start after adding mods

1. Open the **Console** and read the last lines of output — they often name the mod causing trouble.
2. Remove the most recently added or mismatched mod, then restart.
3. Still broken? Restore the backup you took earlier from the **Backups** tab.

For more help, see [My server won't start](/knowledge-base/server-wont-start).

Still stuck? Open a support ticket and our team will help.

title: Invoices, renewals & payment methods
body:
Everything you rent at ReFx Hosting renews automatically, and your invoices and payment methods all live in one place under **Billing**.

## How automatic renewals work

Each game server, voice server, or web hosting plan renews on the billing interval you chose at checkout — Weekly, Biweekly, Monthly, Quarterly, 6 months, or Yearly. Longer terms cost less per month.

When a renewal comes due:

1. An invoice is raised for the next term.
2. We apply any account credit first, then charge your saved payment method.
3. Once payment clears, your service keeps running without interruption.

> Tip: Choosing a longer interval lowers your effective monthly price and means fewer renewals to think about.

## Viewing and paying invoices

Open **Billing** from the left navigation to see every invoice on your account.

To pay an invoice manually:

1. Go to **Billing** and find the invoice.
2. Open it to review the line items and total.
3. Choose your payment method and confirm.

You can also apply a coupon, redeem a gift card, or use account credit when you pay. See [Coupons, gift cards & credit](/knowledge-base/coupons-gift-cards-and-credit) for details.

## Managing payment methods

ReFx Hosting accepts card payments (Stripe) and PayPal. Under **Billing** you can:

- Add or update a card, or link PayPal.
- Set the method used for automatic renewals.
- Remove a method you no longer use.

Keeping a valid method on file is the easiest way to ensure renewals go through without you lifting a finger.

## Account credit

Any credit on your account is shown under **Billing** and is applied automatically toward invoices before we charge a card or PayPal. It's a handy buffer for renewals.

## Unpaid invoices and suspension

If a renewal can't be paid, the invoice stays open and your service may be **suspended** until it's settled.

To bring a suspended service back online:

1. Open **Billing** and locate the unpaid invoice.
2. Update your payment method if the previous charge failed.
3. Pay the invoice.

Once payment clears, the service is reactivated.

> Note: To avoid surprises, keep a current payment method on file and watch for invoice emails as your renewal date approaches.

---

## Related help

- [Upgrading or downgrading your plan](/knowledge-base/upgrading-or-downgrading-your-plan)
- [Coupons, gift cards & credit](/knowledge-base/coupons-gift-cards-and-credit)
